full_response:
In tensor notation, the position of indices indicates the type of tensor. A lower index denotes a covariant index, while an upper index denotes a contravariant index. The rank is determined by the number of indices.

- \(A_a\) has one lower index, so it is a rank-1 covariant tensor, also known as a 1-form.
- \(B^a\) has one upper index, so it is a rank-1 contravariant tensor, also known as a vector.
- \(C_{ab}\) has two lower indices, so it is a rank-2 covariant tensor.

Thus, \(B^a\) is a vector, and \(A_a\) is a 1-form.

\boxed{A_a \text{ is a 1-form (rank-1 covariant tensor); } B^a \text{ is a vector (rank-1 contravariant tensor); } C_{ab} \text{ is a rank-2 covariant tensor.}}
Confidence: 100%
extracted_answer: A_a \text{ is a 1-form (rank-1 covariant tensor); } B^a \text{ is a vector (rank-1 contravariant tensor); } C_{ab} \text{ is a rank-2 covariant tensor.}
